parsed_review.summary: CP00-384 (RP12.P07.M05.S15-RP12.P07.M06.S02, risk A, 10 units; micros "Permission And Audit Binding":8 + "Synthetic Fixture Set":2; deliverables hermes_evidence:2/fixture:1/test:2/security_audit:1/claude_review:1/implementation:1/failure_recovery:2) is a clean descriptor-only billing pack that passes read-only review with one non-blocking P3. The live plan (closeout-pack-plan.json, CP00-384 as actual_first_planned_pack_id, source_ledger_sha 13c7ed65..., deviation_from_plan:false), manifest plan_binding_snapshot, contract projection (contracts/billing-core-contract.json p07_permission_fixture_slice_descriptor + cp384_requirements + cp384_no_write_attestation), README #cp00-384, and source (registry.js BILLING_CORE_CP384_PACK_BINDING/REQUIREMENTS/NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION; service.js createBillingCoreCp384* factories; validators.js coverage+slice validators) are mutually consistent. All 10 rows are descriptor_only:true, runtime_execution:false, customer_safe_errors_only:true, raw_payload_included:false; the validator (scripts/validate-rp12-billing-core-contract.mjs) deep-equals the contract's embedded descriptor against the code-generated descriptor and asserts coverage.valid/slice.valid, contract drift, and handoff targets (to CP00-385 / RP12.P07.M06.S03). All 7 required public exports exist (2 in service.js, 5 in validators.js) and are re-exported via the index.js wildcard (index.js needed no edit). Grep confirms zero IO/network/SQL/runtime paths in packages/billing/src (no fs/readFile/child_process/fetch/http/SQL) — only frozen descriptor factories and false attestation flags; Hermes usage is confined to descriptor evidence-packet identifiers (H12.CP00-384..., emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false), not product runtime. Authority boundary holds: claude_is_final_approval:false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust:false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening:true, runtime_opening_pack_id:CP00-385; manifest production_ready:false/runtime_ready:false is an honest descriptor-only posture and production_ready_candidate is correctly scoped to coverage.valid && slice.valid. Review-hardening evidence is sound: auth preflight passed with valid JSON (exit 0, 1434 bytes), 24 command-evidence entries all exit_code 0 (incl. npm test, npm run validate, npm run build, git diff --check), prompt 2237<25600 bytes, tools restricted to Read/Grep/Glob (forbidden_tools_present:false), summary-only correctly disallowed (security_audit + review-evidence deliverables present), and invalid_review_blockers enumerate the CP178+ rejections; no invalid attempts recorded. The LDIP invalid-attempt file and other dirty docs are correctly quarantined as unrelated and were not treated as CP00-384 evidence; the bundled new-session-handoff.md was inspected and contains no system-reminder injection in its current form. Post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json, raw-output.json, review-receipt.json) are not yet present, which is expected since they follow this read-only step in the hardening_sequence and are not gated by this review.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json double-classifies two pack files as both implementation and unrelated
findings.details: docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md and docs/closeout-packs/cp00-384/ appear simultaneously in untracked_implementation_files (lines 131-132) and in unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 137-138), which are contradictory categories. The manifest's worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (docs/closeout-packs/cp00-384/manifest.json:549-557) does NOT list these two, correctly treating cp00-384/ as the pack's own evidence directory and the handoff as pack-related, so the two generated artifacts disagree. No wrong inference results: active_pack_implementation_refs lists only the billing files, active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty, and the handoff doc is not referenced by any unit's implementation_refs/evidence_refs. Impact is limited to scope-metadata cosmetics; it does not touch the billing implementation, descriptor-only/no-write boundaries, authority boundary, command evidence, or any closeout gate.
findings.recommendation: In the scope generator, make untracked_implementation_files and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved mutually exclusive (or rename the latter to 'all_preserved_dirty_files') and align it with the manifest's worktree_transition_policy so pack-owned paths (docs/closeout-packs/cp00-384/) are not also labeled 'unrelated'. No code or contract change required for CP00-384 closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking informational nit in generated review-scope metadata; does not affect production_ready and may be deferred. Pack closeout and goal closeout are not blocked.
